cadence: five AI insight posts per day; one targeted documented research post plus four rotating research facts across 8bit, NHS, BYA, and ADB

items.asset_brief: Small source-backed stat card: role count, top tags, sample roles, operator question.

source_errors.nhs_profile: HTTP Error 402: Payment Required; profile page fallback failed: HTTP Error 404: Not Found

items.funnel: AI Dev Board data, Not Human Search agent-readiness check, or 8bit research follow-up

x.copy:
Looking at public AI hiring as a market signal: @Waymo has 103 indexed AI/ML roles on AI Dev Board, with current tags clustering around autonomous-vehicles, llm, generative-ai. Avg published salary: $255,000.

The read is not just hiring volume. It points to robotics evaluation, prediction systems, simulation, and safety-critical deployment. What is the cleaner proxy for readiness: model quality, scenario coverage, or operational monitoring?

Data: https://aidevboard.com/company/waymo

linkedin.copy:
Looking at @Waymo through the public hiring data:

1. 103 indexed AI/ML roles in AI Dev Board.
2. Current sample tags cluster around autonomous-vehicles, llm, generative-ai, data-pipeline.
3. Sample roles include Machine Learning Engineer, Depot Automation; Software Engineer, Marketplace ML Platform; Senior Staff Engineer, DevAI.
4. Avg published salary in the indexed sample: $255,000.

The useful read is where the operational work is moving: robotics evaluation, prediction systems, simulation, and safety-critical deployment.

Waymo is a useful public lens on this area. What is the cleaner proxy for readiness: model quality, scenario coverage, or operational monitoring?

Data:
https://aidevboard.com/company/waymo

quality_gate: target company or influential person is named/tagged naturally but never as the first token
documented sources are attached
uses live ADB or NHS stats
does not negatively call out the target
includes a useful operator question or recommendation
flags longform paper opportunity when the stat is large enough

items.asset_brief: Cost stack: data, integration, evals, observability, maintenance, model fees.

x.copy:
Little fact from the 8bit research stack: model API fees are usually 10-20% of the AI build cost. The rest is data plumbing, integration, evals, observability, and maintenance.

Source: https://8bitconcepts.com/research/the-integration-tax.html

linkedin.copy:
Little fact from the 8bit research stack: model API fees are usually 10-20% of an AI build. The bigger lines are data pipelines, system integration, evaluation, observability, and maintenance.

That is why the budget conversation has to happen before the model-choice conversation.

Source:
https://8bitconcepts.com/research/the-integration-tax.html

quality_gate: one small verified fact
source-backed by 8bit research or case-study page
routes to the relevant product or paper
no sales-first CTA
materially different fact_key from previous posts

x.copy:
Little fact from the NHS MCP audit: developer tools had 1,686 indexed sites. Health had 72. Education had 28. The agent ecosystem is crowded in tools and thin in regulated verticals.

Source: https://8bitconcepts.com/research/q2-2026-mcp-ecosystem-health.html

linkedin.copy:
Little fact from the NHS MCP audit: developer tools had 1,686 indexed sites. Health had 72. Education had 28.

The agent ecosystem is crowded around tools and still thin in regulated verticals where the workflow value is high.

Source:
https://8bitconcepts.com/research/q2-2026-mcp-ecosystem-health.html

items.asset_brief: Privacy boundary: remote discovery, local movement.

x.copy:
Little fact from the BYA case study: the remote MCP surface has 4 tools and accepts no harness files, GitHub handles, generated memories, API keys, or file contents. The actual move runs locally.

Source: https://8bitconcepts.com/case-studies.html#bringyour

linkedin.copy:
Little fact from the BYA case study: the remote MCP surface has 4 tools and accepts no harness files, GitHub handles, generated memories, API keys, or file contents.

The product boundary is the point: discovery can happen through an agent surface, but private context moves locally.

Source:
https://8bitconcepts.com/case-studies.html#bringyour

x.copy:
Little fact from the ADB skill data: agents appeared in 2,437 indexed roles, but distributed-systems paid higher on average: $252,318 vs $229,919.

Source: https://8bitconcepts.com/research/q2-2026-ai-compensation-by-skill.html

linkedin.copy:
Little fact from the ADB skill data: agents appeared in 2,437 indexed roles, but distributed-systems paid higher on average: $252,318 vs $229,919.

Volume and negotiating leverage are not the same signal.

Source:
